short: The repeating cycle in which an agent reasons, acts, observes, and continues.
definition: An agentic loop is the core execution pattern of an AI agent. On each iteration the model takes in the current context — goal, history, and recent tool results — produces reasoning and a chosen action, executes that action, and observes the output, repeating until a stopping condition is met. Loop design matters: a poorly bounded loop can spin indefinitely, accumulate context, or fail silently.
